raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"A general evaluative prompt invites assessment of the thesis, gap, and contribution framing in the paper excerpt.","R1.2":"The delegation implies checking whether the motivation, method description, baselines, results, and positioning form a coherent argument.","R1.3":"Because the excerpt reports ECE, WER, and figure-based claims, evaluating whether those reported results support the stated claims is central.","R1.4":"The artifact makes explicit novelty and strongest-baseline claims, so estimating contribution delta versus prior work is expected.","R1.6":"A broad reaction to a draft naturally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.","R1.7":"The excerpt relies on named prior works and literature positioning, making attribution and coverage part of the expected responsibility.","RX.1":"Any projection of feedback responsibility should preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the excerpt alone.","RX.2":"The responsibility includes avoiding endorsement of claims such as firstness or strongest baseline beyond the available evidence.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting the requested responsibilities rather than performing the requested evaluation.","RX.4":"The projection should account for potential downstream harm from overconfident ASR or agent-routing claims without acting on them.","RX.5":"The mapping should remain traceable to specific elements of the delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: A general evaluative prompt invites assessment of the thesis, gap, and contribution framing in the paper excerpt.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation implies checking whether the motivation, method description, baselines, results, and positioning form a coherent argument.
rationale.R1.3: Because the excerpt reports ECE, WER, and figure-based claims, evaluating whether those reported results support the stated claims is central.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes explicit novelty and strongest-baseline claims, so estimating contribution delta versus prior work is expected.
rationale.R1.6: A broad reaction to a draft naturally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.R1.7: The excerpt relies on named prior works and literature positioning, making attribution and coverage part of the expected responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of feedback responsibility should preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The responsibility includes avoiding endorsement of claims such as firstness or strongest baseline beyond the available evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting the requested responsibilities rather than performing the requested evaluation.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for potential downstream harm from overconfident ASR or agent-routing claims without acting on them.
rationale.RX.5: The mapping should remain traceable to specific elements of the delegation and artifact.
